commands.repair_reason: Maintainers should decide or request a retargeted fix because the current branch reverses shipped gateway scoping and lacks real behavior proof.; Needs attention: The diff broadens a gateway data-access scope that was previously narrowed as security hardening.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve the scoped usage default — src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts:883 (sha=01060fcc332a615d4763917746f45132ffdd4613)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `sessions.usage` so list requests without `agentId` return all configured agents' sessions and updates the gateway usage test to expect that behavior.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests 0. Total +2 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the linked bug by source inspection: current main's Usage UI omits `agentId`, while the gateway defaults omitted requests to the default agent. I did not run a live multi-agent Control UI reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers should decide or request a retargeted fix because the current branch reverses shipped gateway scoping and lacks real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(usage): return all agent sessions when no agentId specified
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87132.

When no  is specified in the  request, the backend previously defaulted to returning only the default agent's sessions (usually "main"). This caused the Web UI's agent filter to only show main agent sessions, making it impossible to filter by other agents.

## Changes

- When  is omitted and no specific session key is provided,  is now  instead of defaulting to the configured default agent
- When  is undefined, skip the  step so all agents' sessions are returned
- Updated test to verify that sessions from all configured agents are returned when no agentId is specified

## Test Plan

- Updated existing test to expect all agent sessions returned when no agentId is specified
- Verified existing tests for specific agentId filtering still pass

Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87132
target.author: jefsky
